title: Thlaspi pulvinatum
intro: Thlaspi pulvinatum, also known as Balkan penny cress, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Brassicaceae family, characterized by its small, white, and four-petaled flowers. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and other conditions. The bioactive constituents of Thlaspi pulvinatum include isothiocyanates, glucosinolates, and other compounds that are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Balkan penny cress is used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as in traditional medicine to support overall well-being and reduce stress.
taxonomy: Thlaspi pulvinatum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae, commonly known as the mustard or cabbage family. This family includes a wide range of plants, such as broccoli, cauliflower, and kale. Thlaspi pulvinatum is further classified within the genus Thlaspi, which consists of annual and biennial species native to Europe. The plant's classification also includes the order Brassicales and the kingdom Plantae. This classification reflects the plant's evolutionary relationships with other Brassicaceae species.
